system.description: Ships made in Avalon tend to be smaller and built for speed. If your Ship is from Avalon, it is most likely a brig, brigantine, or schooner.
system.bonus: Gain two Bonus Dice for any Risk involving speed and maneuverability — such as trying to flat outrun another Ship.

system.description: Most Castillian Ships are large and square-rigged with three decks and three masts. Difficult to take down, but very slow, requiring tons of canvas to move. Your Ship is probably a galleon or a man-of-war.
system.bonus: Your Ship can take one additional Hit before it takes each Critical Hit (6 Hits per Critical, instead of 5).

system.description: While Eisen Ships are otherwise unnotable, they do have the most dangerous marines in all of Théah.
system.bonus: Your Ship has 5 additional Crew, and your Crew can now divide into up to 3 Squads.

system.description: Montaigne Ships have one thing in common: they are all beautiful. They're also usually well armed to protect all that beauty. Your Ship is probably a frigate or a schooner.
system.bonus: When making a social Risk (such as negotiating with other Ships), you gain two Bonus Dice.

system.description: Sarmatia has a very small fleet, and frankly, most of their Ships were "acquired" from other Nations.
Ussura's Ships are designed to be sailed in northern, icy waters and as such they tend to be hardy and strong.
system.bonus: In extremely choppy waters, during a storm, while sailing in dangerous waters (an icy ocean, reef-infested waters), or when otherwise under threat by the environment, gain two Bonus Dice.

system.description: Vodacce Ships are built for the long haul: four masts, a huge hull. They are merchant vessels designed for sailing to distant lands.
Almost all Vesten Ships are fluyts, designed for long travel, but with more sophisticated sails, reducing the number of Crew needed.
system.bonus: When a Vesten Ship raids another vessel for Wealth, it gains all of the other Ship's Wealth rather than half.

system.description: Your Ship was designed and built outside of Théah, such as the Crescent Empire, Ifri or Cathay.
system.bonus: An Exotic Ship always earns one more Wealth whenever it sells foreign goods in Théah, or whenever it sells Théan goods in a foreign port.
